speaker: doctor
text: After reviewing your tests, it appears you have coronary artery disease. We'll need to manage this carefully to prevent any complications.

speaker: doctor
text: Your heart rate is elevated at 128 beats per minute, which is higher than normal. Are you experiencing any palpitations or chest discomfort?

speaker: doctor
text: Your systolic blood pressure is measuring at 121 mmHg, which is within a reasonable range. How have you been feeling lately?

speaker: patient
text: I feel generally okay, but I've noticed I'm urinating much more frequently than usual.

speaker: doctor
text: Your body temperature is currently 36.9 degrees Celsius, indicating no fever at the moment.

speaker: patient
text: Yes, it's been quite bothersome. The increased urination started about a week ago.

speaker: doctor
text: That symptom, polyuria, can be related to blood sugar issues or even medication side effects. Let me check your diastolic blood pressure as well.

speaker: patient
text: Apart from the urination, I haven't noticed any other new symptoms.

speaker: doctor
text: Your diastolic blood pressure is 64 mmHg, which is on the lower side but still acceptable. Given your condition, I am going to start you on insulin glargine, 16 units at night, to help control your blood sugar.

speaker: patient
text: I see. Will this insulin affect my energy levels or cause any side effects?

speaker: doctor
text: Some patients experience mild side effects like low blood sugar if meals are skipped, but we'll monitor you closely. Do you have any history of hypoglycemia?

speaker: patient
text: No, I haven't had low blood sugar episodes before.

speaker: doctor
text: Good. We'll also keep an eye on your heart rate. How have you been feeling in terms of fatigue or shortness of breath?

speaker: patient
text: Sometimes I feel a bit tired, but nothing too severe.

speaker: doctor
text: Actually, I noticed your body temperature has increased to 38.2 degrees Celsius. Are you feeling feverish or experiencing chills now?

speaker: patient
text: No, I actually feel fine and my temperature was 36.9 earlier today.

speaker: doctor
text: Also, your heart rate is now 130 beats per minute, slightly higher than before. Have you been more anxious or physically active recently?

speaker: patient
text: Not really, I was resting when you measured it.

speaker: patient
text: By the way, I checked my blood pressure myself and it was 121 mmHg systolic, matching your earlier reading.

speaker: doctor
text: That's consistent, which is good. Given the situation, I am going to switch your medication to atorvastatin 20mg at night to better manage your cholesterol levels along with your coronary artery disease.
